response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned software engineer with strong distributed systems and event-driven architecture experience, but the role's core requirement — substantial C++ development — is essentially unmet. His C++ exposure is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project; his primary stack across 10+ years is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 security standards, MQTT, or any of the industrial protocols listed as advantageous. The gap between the candidate's actual expertise and the role's technical core is too wide to bridge.
strengths: Proven high-performance, low-latency distributed systems work (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/second with async I/O and fiber-based concurrency)
Event-driven and message-broker architectural experience through the open-source GISCollective platform and microservices history
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing through workshops, code katas, and public talks — relevant to the team-mentorship expectations of the role
concerns: C++ experience is limited to a single six-month role and a bachelor thesis; it is absent from the skills section and not present in any work after 2015
No industrial automation, IoT, or OT security background; no familiarity with IEC 62443, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, or any of the industrial protocols listed
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) implementation, application security risk management, or cybersecurity practices relevant to industrial systems
